# Discussion — Comparing CPR and eDNA: Dimensions of Diversity

The **Continuous Plankton Recorder (CPR)** and the **RoCSI eDNA metabarcoding** represent fundamentally different ways of observing plankton communities. Rather than asking whether these approaches agree or disagree, a more informative question is at what ecological and taxonomic scales they converge—and where their comparability breaks down.

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(phyloseq)
library(ggVennDiagram)

ps_18s <- readRDS("data/ps_18s.rds")
ps_coi <- readRDS("data/ps_coi.rds")
ps_cpr <- readRDS("data/ps_cpr.rds")

get_phyla <- function(ps) {
  tt <- tax_table(ps)

  # pull the Phylum column (as character), drop NA/blank/"NA"
  ph <- as.character(tt[, "Phylum"])
  ph <- ph[!is.na(ph) & ph != "" & ph != "NA"]

  sort(unique(ph))
}

phyla_sets <- list(
  `18S` = get_phyla(ps_18s),
  `COI` = get_phyla(ps_coi),
  `CPR` = get_phyla(ps_cpr)
)

# quick counts
sapply(phyla_sets, length)

```

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Venn diagram
ggVennDiagram(phyla_sets, label_alpha = 0) +
  ggplot2::labs(title = "Shared phyla across datasets")

```

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(tidyr)
library(knitr)

shared_all <- Reduce(intersect, phyla_sets)
only_18s   <- setdiff(phyla_sets$`18S`, union(phyla_sets$`COI`, phyla_sets$`CPR`))
only_coi   <- setdiff(phyla_sets$`COI`, union(phyla_sets$`18S`, phyla_sets$`CPR`))
only_cpr   <- setdiff(phyla_sets$`CPR`, union(phyla_sets$`18S`, phyla_sets$`COI`))

phyla_out <- tibble(
  Category = c("Shared by all", "Only 18S", "Only COI", "Only CPR"),
  `# Phyla` = c(length(shared_all), length(only_18s), length(only_coi), length(only_cpr)),
  Phyla = c(
    paste(sort(shared_all), collapse = ", "),
    paste(sort(only_18s), collapse = ", "),
    paste(sort(only_coi), collapse = ", "),
    paste(sort(only_cpr), collapse = ", ")
  )
)

knitr::kable(phyla_out, align = c("l", "r", "l"))

```

Let's now merge that 2 eDNA metabarcoding datasets:

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

# 1) Make taxa IDs unique across markers
taxa_names(ps_18s) <- paste0("18S__", taxa_names(ps_18s))
taxa_names(ps_coi) <- paste0("COI__", taxa_names(ps_coi))

# 2) Add a marker column in the tax_table
add_marker <- function(ps, marker) {
  tt <- as(tax_table(ps), "matrix")
  tt <- cbind(tt, marker = marker)
  tax_table(ps) <- tax_table(tt)
  ps
}
ps_18s <- add_marker(ps_18s, "18S")
ps_coi <- add_marker(ps_coi, "COI")

# 3) Ensure tax_table columns match (pad missing cols with NA)
cols_all <- union(colnames(tax_table(ps_18s)), colnames(tax_table(ps_coi)))

pad_tax <- function(ps, cols_all) {
  tt <- as(tax_table(ps), "matrix")
  miss <- setdiff(cols_all, colnames(tt))
  if (length(miss) > 0) {
    tt <- cbind(tt, matrix(NA, nrow = nrow(tt), ncol = length(miss),
                          dimnames = list(rownames(tt), miss)))
  }
  tt <- tt[, cols_all, drop = FALSE]
  tax_table(ps) <- tax_table(tt)
  ps
}
ps_18s <- pad_tax(ps_18s, cols_all)
ps_coi <- pad_tax(ps_coi, cols_all)

# 4) Merge (stacks taxa/features)
ps_edna <- merge_phyloseq(ps_18s, ps_coi)

ps_edna
table(tax_table(ps_edna)[, "marker"])
```
```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)

sample_data(ps_edna)$Water_masses <- ifelse(sample_data(ps_edna)$lon < -10,
                                       "Open Ocean", "Shelf")

# Make sure Water_masses is a factor (optional, but useful for consistent plotting)
sample_data(ps_edna)$Water_masses <- factor(sample_data(ps_edna)$Water_masses, 
                                       levels = c("Open Ocean", "Shelf"))

ord <- ordinate(ps_edna, method = "PCoA", distance = "bray")

plot_ordination(ps_edna, ord, color = "Water_masses") +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = Water_masses),
               type = "norm",
               linetype = "dashed",
               size = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#1F4E79", "#2E8B57")) +
  ggtitle("Ordination – RoCSI eDNA - water masses") +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
sample_data(ps_edna)$cpr <- factor(sample_data(ps_edna)$cpr)

ord <- ordinate(ps_edna, method = "PCoA", distance = "bray")

plot_ordination(ps_edna, ord, color = "cpr") +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = cpr),
               type = "norm",
               linetype = "dashed",
               size = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#1B9E77", "#D95F02", "#7570B3", "#E7298A")) +
  ggtitle("Ordination – RoCSI eDNA") +
  theme_minimal()
```

### For poster
```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}

sample_data(ps_edna)$cpr <- factor(sample_data(ps_edna)$cpr)

p <- plot_ordination(ps_edna, ord, color = "cpr") +
  geom_point(size = 6, alpha = 0.9) +
  stat_ellipse(
    aes(group = cpr),
    type = "norm",
    linetype = "solid",
    linewidth = 1.4
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("#1B9E77", "#D95F02", "#7570B3", "#E7298A"),
    name = "Transect"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "RoCSI eDNA ordination",
    subtitle = "Points = samples; ellipses = 95% normal confidence"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 22) +
  theme(
    # Make it “poster bold”
    plot.title = element_text(size = 34, face = "bold", hjust = 0),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0),

    axis.title = element_text(size = 28, face = "bold"),
    axis.text  = element_text(size = 22),

    legend.title = element_text(size = 26, face = "bold"),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 22),

    # Put legend where it won't cover data (adjust as needed)
    legend.position = "right",

    # Increase spacing / clarity
    panel.grid.major = element_line(linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),

    # Make it print-friendly
    axis.line = element_line(linewidth = 1, colour = "black"),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA)
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 8, alpha = 1)))

p
```


### compare presence/absence in eDNA vs CPR

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
pa_transform <- function(ps) {
  ps_pa <- ps
  otu_table(ps_pa) <- otu_table((otu_table(ps) > 0) * 1, taxa_are_rows(ps))
  ps_pa
}

ps_edna_pa <- pa_transform(ps_edna)
ps_cpr_pa  <- pa_transform(ps_cpr)

# 2 get taxa present at a given taxonomic rank

get_taxa_at_rank <- function(ps, rank) {
  tt <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps))
  
  # taxa present at least once
  present_taxa <- taxa_names(ps)[taxa_sums(ps) > 0]
  
  # extract taxonomic labels
  taxa <- tt[present_taxa, rank]
  
  taxa <- taxa[!is.na(taxa) & taxa != ""]
  unique(as.character(taxa))
}

# 3 Compare eDNA vs CPR across taxonomic levels
ranks_to_test <- intersect(rank_names(ps_edna), rank_names(ps_cpr))

comparison <- lapply(ranks_to_test, function(rank) {
  edna_taxa <- get_taxa_at_rank(ps_edna_pa, rank)
  cpr_taxa  <- get_taxa_at_rank(ps_cpr_pa,  rank)

  data.frame(
    rank = rank,
    n_edna = length(edna_taxa),
    n_cpr  = length(cpr_taxa),
    n_shared = length(intersect(edna_taxa, cpr_taxa)),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})

taxa_overlap_summary <- do.call(rbind, comparison)

taxa_overlap_summary$perc_shared_edna <- with(
  taxa_overlap_summary,
  100 * n_shared / n_edna
)

taxa_overlap_summary$perc_shared_cpr <- with(
  taxa_overlap_summary,
  100 * n_shared / n_cpr
)

# optional: round for nicer tables
taxa_overlap_summary$perc_shared_edna <- round(taxa_overlap_summary$perc_shared_edna, 1)
taxa_overlap_summary$perc_shared_cpr  <- round(taxa_overlap_summary$perc_shared_cpr, 1)

knitr::kable(
  taxa_overlap_summary,
  caption = "Taxonomic overlap between eDNA and CPR datasets (presence/absence).",
  digits = 1
) |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)

```


::: {.callout-note}
eDNA detects far more taxonomic diversity than CPR, but overlap drops rapidly with taxonomic resolution; CPR diversity is largely a subset of eDNA at high ranks, while species-level agreement is essentially zero.
:::

### Stacked barplot from table

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)

# Create stacked-bar input from your existing summary table
overlap_plot_df <- taxa_overlap_summary %>%
  mutate(
    edna_only = n_edna - n_shared,
    cpr_only  = n_cpr - n_shared
  ) %>%
  select(rank, shared = n_shared, `CPR only` = cpr_only, `eDNA only` = edna_only) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(shared, `CPR only`, `eDNA only`),
    names_to = "category",
    values_to = "n_taxa"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    rank = factor(rank, levels = c("Kingdom", "Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus", "Species")),
    category = factor(category, levels = c("shared", "CPR only", "eDNA only"))
  )

# Stacked barplot
p_overlap <- ggplot(overlap_plot_df, aes(x = rank, y = n_taxa, fill = category)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.75) +
  labs(
    x = "Taxonomic rank",
    y = "Number of taxa",
    fill = NULL,
    title = "Taxonomic overlap between eDNA and CPR datasets"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

p_overlap

```

### Quantifying agreement using similarity metrics per rank (Jaccard similarity)

Jaccard similarity was chosen to compare eDNA and CPR datasets because it provides a simple measure of shared taxa using presence–absence information

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
taxa_overlap_summary$jaccard <- with(
  taxa_overlap_summary,
  n_shared / (n_edna + n_cpr - n_shared)
)

rank_order <- c("Kingdom","Phylum","Class","Order","Family","Genus","Species")
taxa_overlap_summary$rank <- factor(taxa_overlap_summary$rank, levels = rank_order)

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  subset(taxa_overlap_summary, rank != "Species"),
  aes(x = rank, y = jaccard)
) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Taxonomic rank",
    y = "Jaccard similarity (presence/absence)",
    title = "Jaccard similarity between eDNA and CPR across taxonomic ranks",
    subtitle = "Species level excluded"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

```

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
present_taxlabels <- function(ps, rank) {
  tt <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps))
  present_asvs <- taxa_names(ps)[taxa_sums(ps) > 0]
  labs <- tt[present_asvs, rank]

  labs <- as.character(labs)
  labs <- labs[!is.na(labs) & labs != ""]
  sort(unique(labs))
}

ranks_to_test <- intersect(rank_names(ps_edna_pa), rank_names(ps_cpr_pa))

rank_taxa_table <- do.call(
  rbind,
  lapply(ranks_to_test, function(rank) {

    edna_taxa <- present_taxlabels(ps_edna_pa, rank)
    cpr_taxa  <- present_taxlabels(ps_cpr_pa,  rank)
    shared    <- intersect(edna_taxa, cpr_taxa)

    data.frame(
      rank   = rank,
      eDNA   = if (length(edna_taxa) > 0) paste(edna_taxa, collapse = ", ") else NA,
      CPR    = if (length(cpr_taxa)  > 0) paste(cpr_taxa,  collapse = ", ") else NA,
      Shared = if (length(shared)    > 0) paste(shared,    collapse = ", ") else NA,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })
)

```

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(gt)

rank_taxa_table |>
  (\(x) x[seq_len(match("Order", x$rank)), ])() |>
  gt(rowname_col = "rank") |>

  # Labels
  cols_label(
    eDNA   = "eDNA taxa",
    CPR    = "CPR taxa",
    Shared = "Shared taxa"
  ) |>

  # Header
  tab_header(
    title = "Rank-by-rank taxonomic composition (presence/absence)",
    subtitle = "Comparison between eDNA and CPR datasets"
  ) |>

  # Improve text handling
  cols_width(
    eDNA   ~ px(350),
    CPR    ~ px(350),
    Shared ~ px(350)
  ) |>

  # Wrap long comma-separated lists
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(whitespace = "normal"),
    locations = cells_body(everything())
  ) |>

  # Make rank labels stand out
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_stub()
  ) |>

  # Subtle table aesthetics
  opt_all_caps() |>
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = "small",
    data_row.padding = px(6),
    heading.title.font.size = 16,
    heading.subtitle.font.size = 12
  )

```

**Table: Notable copepod genera identified from COI (eDNA) and CPR datasets.**

| Genus          | Why Notable                                                | Shelf vs Open‑Ocean Pattern (<10° vs >10° longitude) | Evidence Source (COI / CPR)       |
|----------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------|
| Paracalanus    | Most consistently abundant across longitudes                | Broad, present in both regions                        | COI + CPR                          |
| Clausocalanus  | Strong offshore abundance                                   | Open ocean (>10°)                                     | COI + CPR                          |
| Nannocalanus   | Prominent peaks in offshore COI samples                     | Open ocean (>10°)                                     | COI + CPR                          |
| Centropages    | Clear blocks near shelf samples                             | Shelf (<10°)                                          | COI + CPR                          |
| Acartia        | Coastal‑type genus, repeating in shelf regions              | Shelf (<10°)                                          | COI + CPR                          |
| Calanus        | Dominant in CPR at several shelf/slope stations             | Shelf–slope                                           | CPR only (not in COI top‑10)       |
| Temora         | Distinct CPR occurrences in shelf waters                    | Shelf (<10°)                                          | CPR                                |
| Metridia       | Offshore oceanic signature in CPR                           | Open ocean (>10°)                                     | CPR                                |
| Oithona        | High relative abundance in COI, minimal CPR representation  | Broad, more offshore in COI                           | COI                                |
| Oncaea         | COI‑specific peaks in offshore waters                       | Open ocean (>10°)                                     | COI                                |
``

::: {.callout-note}
### 💬 Discussion
- Agreement across methods: Paracalanus, Clausocalanus, Nannocalanus, Centropages show consistent patterns in both plots (first four rows), giving confidence they are genuinely abundant and/or habitat‑associated as indicated.
_ CPR‑leaning taxa: Calanus, Temora, Metridia are clearly visible in CPR but not in the COI top‑10 figure—likely reflecting net/ID strengths for these morphologically distinctive, larger calanoids.
_ eDNA‑leaning taxa: Oithona, Oncaea appear strongly in COI but are minimal in CPR—consistent with CPR under‑capturing small/fragile forms and/or taxonomic resolution limits.

:::

### Combined figures

#### zooplankton

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ---- genera to plot ----
genera10 <- c(
  "Paracalanus","Clausocalanus","Nannocalanus","Centropages","Acartia",
  "Calanus","Temora","Metridia","Oithona","Oncaea"
)

# ---- palette + named colours for the 10 genera ----
my_colors <- c("#FF6666", "#8DD3C7", "#377EB8", "#4DAF4A",
               "#984EA3", "#A6D854", "#FFFF33", "#FF7F00",
               "#F781BF", "#999999")

genus_cols <- setNames(my_colors[1:length(genera10)], genera10)

# ---- helper: phyloseq -> long df with longitude + Genus + PA ----
ps_to_long_df <- function(ps_obj, approach_name, genera_keep, lon_var = NULL, tax_rank = "Genus") {
  stopifnot(inherits(ps_obj, "phyloseq"))

  otu <- as(otu_table(ps_obj), "matrix")
  if (!taxa_are_rows(ps_obj)) otu <- t(otu)
  otu <- (otu > 0) * 1

  tax <- as(tax_table(ps_obj), "matrix") %>% as.data.frame(check.names = FALSE)
  tax$.taxa_id <- rownames(tax)

  sam <- as(sample_data(ps_obj), "data.frame") %>%
    rownames_to_column("SampleID")
  names(sam) <- trimws(names(sam))

  if (is.null(lon_var)) {
    lon_candidates <- names(sam)[grepl("^lon(gitude)?$", tolower(names(sam)))]
    if (length(lon_candidates) == 0) lon_candidates <- names(sam)[grepl("lon|long", tolower(names(sam)))]
    if (length(lon_candidates) == 0) {
      stop(
        "Could not find a longitude column in sample_data(ps_obj).\n",
        "Available columns:\n  ", paste(names(sam), collapse = ", ")
      )
    }
    lon_var <- lon_candidates[1]
  } else {
    if (!lon_var %in% names(sam)) {
      stop(sprintf(
        "Longitude column '%s' not found. Available columns:\n  %s",
        lon_var, paste(names(sam), collapse = ", ")
      ))
    }
  }

  if (!tax_rank %in% names(tax)) {
    stop(sprintf("Tax table column '%s' not found. Available tax ranks:\n  %s",
                 tax_rank, paste(names(tax), collapse = ", ")))
  }

  otu_long <- as.data.frame(otu) %>%
    rownames_to_column(".taxa_id") %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -".taxa_id", names_to = "SampleID", values_to = "pa")

  otu_long %>%
    left_join(tax, by = ".taxa_id") %>%
    left_join(sam, by = "SampleID") %>%
    mutate(
      Genus = as.character(.data[[tax_rank]]),
      approach = approach_name,
      longitude = as.numeric(.data[[lon_var]])
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Genus), Genus %in% genera_keep) %>%
    select(approach, SampleID, longitude, Genus, pa)
}

# ---- transect binning settings ----
lon_min <- -16.149
lon_max <- -1.246
n_bins  <- 10
lon_breaks <- seq(lon_min, lon_max, length.out = n_bins + 1)

# ---- build binned presence/absence by genus (any detection in bin = present) ----
df_binned <- bind_rows(
  ps_to_long_df(ps_cpr_pa,  "CPR",  genera10, lon_var = NULL, tax_rank = "Genus"),
  ps_to_long_df(ps_edna_pa, "eDNA", genera10, lon_var = NULL, tax_rank = "Genus")
) %>%
  filter(!is.na(longitude), longitude >= lon_min, longitude <= lon_max) %>%
  mutate(lon_bin = cut(longitude, breaks = lon_breaks, include.lowest = TRUE, right = TRUE)) %>%
  group_by(approach, lon_bin, Genus) %>%
  summarise(pa = as.integer(any(pa == 1)), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    approach = factor(approach, levels = c("CPR", "eDNA"))
  )

# ---- rank genera by prevalence (most present across bins+approaches = top) ----
genus_rank <- df_binned %>%
  group_by(Genus) %>%
  summarise(prevalence = sum(pa == 1, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(prevalence), Genus)

genus_levels <- genus_rank$Genus

# apply ordering + fill mapping
df_binned <- df_binned %>%
  mutate(
    Genus = factor(Genus, levels = genus_levels),
    fill_genus = ifelse(pa == 1, as.character(Genus), NA)
  )

# reorder colours to match ranked genera
genus_cols_ranked <- genus_cols[genus_levels]

# ---- x-axis labels: bin midpoints ----
bin_mids <- (lon_breaks[-1] + lon_breaks[-length(lon_breaks)]) / 2
mid_labels <- sprintf("%.2f", bin_mids)
names(mid_labels) <- levels(df_binned$lon_bin)

# ---- plot (match phytoplankton style) ----
p <- ggplot(df_binned, aes(x = lon_bin, y = Genus, fill = fill_genus)) +
  geom_tile(width = 0.95, height = 0.9, colour = "grey80") +
  facet_wrap(~ approach, ncol = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = genus_cols_ranked,
    na.value = "white",
    breaks = genus_levels,
    limits = genus_levels,
    drop = FALSE,
    name = "Genus"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = mid_labels) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(genus_levels)) +  # most prevalent at TOP
  labs(x = "Longitude section (bin midpoint)", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y  = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank()
  )

print(p)
```

##### Improved version

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)

# ---- genera to plot ----
genera10 <- c(
  "Paracalanus", "Clausocalanus", "Nannocalanus", "Centropages", "Acartia",
  "Calanus", "Temora", "Metridia", "Oithona", "Oncaea"
)

# ---- palette ----
my_colors <- c(
  "#FF6666", "#8DD3C7", "#377EB8", "#4DAF4A",
  "#984EA3", "#A6D854", "#FFFF33", "#FF7F00",
  "#F781BF", "#999999"
)

genus_cols <- setNames(my_colors[1:length(genera10)], genera10)

# ---- helper: phyloseq -> long df ----
ps_to_long_df <- function(ps_obj, approach_name, genera_keep,
                          lon_var = NULL, tax_rank = "Genus") {
  
  stopifnot(inherits(ps_obj, "phyloseq"))
  
  otu <- as(otu_table(ps_obj), "matrix")
  if (!taxa_are_rows(ps_obj)) otu <- t(otu)
  otu <- (otu > 0) * 1
  
  tax <- as(tax_table(ps_obj), "matrix") %>%
    as.data.frame(check.names = FALSE)
  tax$.taxa_id <- rownames(tax)
  
  sam <- as(sample_data(ps_obj), "data.frame") %>%
    rownames_to_column("SampleID")
  
  names(sam) <- trimws(names(sam))
  
  if (is.null(lon_var)) {
    lon_candidates <- names(sam)[grepl("^lon(gitude)?$", tolower(names(sam)))]
    
    if (length(lon_candidates) == 0) {
      lon_candidates <- names(sam)[grepl("lon|long", tolower(names(sam)))]
    }
    
    if (length(lon_candidates) == 0) {
      stop("Could not find a longitude column in sample_data(ps_obj).")
    }
    
    lon_var <- lon_candidates[1]
  }
  
  if (!tax_rank %in% names(tax)) {
    stop(sprintf(
      "Tax table column '%s' not found. Available ranks are: %s",
      tax_rank, paste(names(tax), collapse = ", ")
    ))
  }
  
  otu_long <- as.data.frame(otu) %>%
    rownames_to_column(".taxa_id") %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = -".taxa_id",
      names_to = "SampleID",
      values_to = "pa"
    )
  
  otu_long %>%
    left_join(tax, by = ".taxa_id") %>%
    left_join(sam, by = "SampleID") %>%
    mutate(
      Genus = as.character(.data[[tax_rank]]),
      approach = approach_name,
      longitude = as.numeric(.data[[lon_var]])
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Genus), Genus %in% genera_keep) %>%
    select(approach, SampleID, longitude, Genus, pa)
}

# ---- longitude binning settings ----
lon_min <- -16.149
lon_max <- -1.246
n_bins  <- 10

lon_breaks <- seq(lon_min, lon_max, length.out = n_bins + 1)

# ---- build long presence/absence dataframe ----
df_long <- bind_rows(
  ps_to_long_df(ps_cpr_pa,  "CPR",  genera10, lon_var = NULL, tax_rank = "Genus"),
  ps_to_long_df(ps_edna_pa, "eDNA", genera10, lon_var = NULL, tax_rank = "Genus")
) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(longitude),
    longitude >= lon_min,
    longitude <= lon_max
  )

# ---- collapse to genus-level presence/absence per sample ----
# Important: avoids counting multiple ASVs from the same genus in the same sample
df_sample_genus_pa <- df_long %>%
  group_by(approach, SampleID, longitude, Genus) %>%
  summarise(
    pa = as.integer(any(pa == 1)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ---- binned presence/absence ----
# A genus is considered present in a longitude bin if detected in any sample in that bin
df_binned <- df_sample_genus_pa %>%
  mutate(
    lon_bin = cut(
      longitude,
      breaks = lon_breaks,
      include.lowest = TRUE,
      right = TRUE
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(approach, lon_bin, Genus) %>%
  summarise(
    pa = as.integer(any(pa == 1)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    approach = factor(approach, levels = c("CPR", "eDNA"))
  )

# ---- prevalence across longitude bins ----
prevalence_df <- df_binned %>%
  group_by(approach, Genus) %>%
  summarise(
    n_present_bins = sum(pa == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_bins = n_distinct(lon_bin),
    prevalence = 100 * n_present_bins / n_bins,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ---- order genera by overall prevalence across both methods ----
genus_levels <- prevalence_df %>%
  group_by(Genus) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_prevalence = mean(prevalence, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_prevalence), Genus) %>%
  pull(Genus)

df_binned <- df_binned %>%
  mutate(
    Genus = factor(Genus, levels = genus_levels),
    fill_genus = ifelse(pa == 1, as.character(Genus), NA)
  )

prevalence_df <- prevalence_df %>%
  mutate(
    Genus = factor(Genus, levels = genus_levels),
    approach = factor(approach, levels = c("CPR", "eDNA"))
  )

genus_cols_ranked <- genus_cols[genus_levels]

# ---- x-axis labels: bin midpoints ----
bin_mids <- (lon_breaks[-1] + lon_breaks[-length(lon_breaks)]) / 2
mid_labels <- sprintf("%.1f", bin_mids)
names(mid_labels) <- levels(df_binned$lon_bin)

# ---- heatmap panel ----
p_heatmap <- ggplot(df_binned, aes(x = lon_bin, y = Genus, fill = fill_genus)) +
  geom_tile(width = 0.95, height = 0.9, colour = "grey80", linewidth = 0.25) +
  facet_wrap(~ approach, ncol = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = genus_cols_ranked,
    na.value = "white",
    breaks = genus_levels,
    limits = genus_levels,
    drop = FALSE,
    name = "Genus"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = mid_labels) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(genus_levels)) +
  labs(
    x = "Longitude section",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

# ---- prevalence barplot panel ----
p_prev <- ggplot(prevalence_df, aes(x = prevalence, y = Genus)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, fill = "grey50") +
  facet_wrap(~ approach, ncol = 1) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(genus_levels)) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    breaks = c(0, 50, 100),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Prevalence across\nlongitude bins (%)",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank()
  )

# ---- combine heatmap and prevalence barplot ----
p_final <- p_heatmap + p_prev +
  plot_layout(widths = c(4.5, 1.2), guides = "collect") +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Copepod genus detection across longitude sections"
  ) &
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14)
  )

p_final
```

#### Phytoplankton

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

# ---- genera to plot ----
families9 <- c(
  "Rhizosoleniaceae",
  "Chaetoceraceae",
  "Ceratiaceae",
  "Coccolithaceae",
  "Dictyochaceae",
  "Leptocylindraceae",
  "Noelaerhabdaceae",
  "Rhabdosphaeraceae",
  "Prorocentraceae"
)

# ---- your palette + named colours for the 10 genera ----
my_colors <- c("#66C2A5", "#FC8D62", "#8DA0CB", "#E78AC3", "#FFD92F",
               "#E5C494", "#B3B3B3", "#A65628","#FFFFB3", "#BEBADA",
               "#FB8072", "#80B1D3", "#FDB462")

fam_cols <- setNames(my_colors[1:length(families9)], families9)

# ---- helper: phyloseq -> long df with longitude + Family + PA ----
ps_to_long_df <- function(ps_obj, approach_name, families_keep, lon_var = NULL, tax_rank = "Family") {
  stopifnot(inherits(ps_obj, "phyloseq"))

  # OTU (taxa x samples) presence/absence
  otu <- as(otu_table(ps_obj), "matrix")
  if (!taxa_are_rows(ps_obj)) otu <- t(otu)
  otu <- (otu > 0) * 1

  # taxonomy
  tax <- as(tax_table(ps_obj), "matrix") %>% as.data.frame(check.names = FALSE)
  tax$.taxa_id <- rownames(tax)

  # sample data
  sam <- as(sample_data(ps_obj), "data.frame") %>%
    rownames_to_column("SampleID")
  names(sam) <- trimws(names(sam))

  # detect longitude column if not provided
  if (is.null(lon_var)) {
    lon_candidates <- names(sam)[grepl("^lon(gitude)?$", tolower(names(sam)))]
    if (length(lon_candidates) == 0) lon_candidates <- names(sam)[grepl("lon|long", tolower(names(sam)))]
    if (length(lon_candidates) == 0) {
      stop(
        "Could not find a longitude column in sample_data(ps_obj).\n",
        "Available columns:\n  ", paste(names(sam), collapse = ", ")
      )
    }
    lon_var <- lon_candidates[1]
  } else {
    if (!lon_var %in% names(sam)) {
      stop(sprintf(
        "Longitude column '%s' not found. Available columns:\n  %s",
        lon_var, paste(names(sam), collapse = ", ")
      ))
    }
  }

  if (!tax_rank %in% names(tax)) {
    stop(sprintf("Tax table column '%s' not found. Available tax ranks:\n  %s",
                 tax_rank, paste(names(tax), collapse = ", ")))
  }

  otu_long <- as.data.frame(otu) %>%
    rownames_to_column(".taxa_id") %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -".taxa_id", names_to = "SampleID", values_to = "pa")

  otu_long %>%
    left_join(tax, by = ".taxa_id") %>%
    left_join(sam, by = "SampleID") %>%
    mutate(
      Family = as.character(.data[[tax_rank]]),
      approach = approach_name,
      longitude = as.numeric(.data[[lon_var]])
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Family), Family %in% families_keep) %>%
    select(approach, SampleID, longitude, Family, pa)
}

# ---- transect binning settings ----
lon_min <- -16.149
lon_max <- -1.246
n_bins  <- 10
lon_breaks <- seq(lon_min, lon_max, length.out = n_bins + 1)

# ---- build binned presence/absence by family (any detection in bin = present) ----
# ---- build binned presence/absence by family (any detection in bin = present) ----
df_binned <- bind_rows(
  ps_to_long_df(ps_cpr_pa,  "CPR",  families9, lon_var = NULL, tax_rank = "Family"),
  ps_to_long_df(ps_edna_pa, "eDNA", families9, lon_var = NULL, tax_rank = "Family")
) %>%
  filter(!is.na(longitude), longitude >= lon_min, longitude <= lon_max) %>%
  mutate(lon_bin = cut(longitude, breaks = lon_breaks, include.lowest = TRUE, right = TRUE)) %>%
  group_by(approach, lon_bin, Family) %>%
  summarise(pa = as.integer(any(pa == 1)), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    approach = factor(approach, levels = c("CPR", "eDNA"))
  )

# ---- rank families (top = most prevalent across bins and approaches) ----
fam_rank <- df_binned %>%
  group_by(Family) %>%
  summarise(
    # prevalence across ALL bins/approaches
    prevalence = sum(pa == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(prevalence), Family)

fam_levels <- fam_rank$Family  # most prevalent first

# ---- apply ordering + fill mapping ----
df_binned <- df_binned %>%
  mutate(
    Family = factor(Family, levels = fam_levels),  # rows/legend order control
    fill_family = ifelse(pa == 1, as.character(Family), NA)
  )

# reorder colours to match the ranked families
fam_cols_ranked <- fam_cols[fam_levels]

# ---- x-axis labels: bin midpoints ----
bin_mids <- (lon_breaks[-1] + lon_breaks[-length(lon_breaks)]) / 2
mid_labels <- sprintf("%.2f", bin_mids)
names(mid_labels) <- levels(df_binned$lon_bin)

# ---- plot ----
p <- ggplot(df_binned, aes(x = lon_bin, y = Family, fill = fill_family)) +
  geom_tile(width = 0.95, height = 0.9, colour = "grey80") +
  facet_wrap(~ approach, ncol = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = fam_cols_ranked,
    na.value = "white",
    breaks = fam_levels,
    limits = fam_levels,
    drop = FALSE,
    name = "Family"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = mid_labels) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(fam_levels)) +
  labs(x = "Longitude section (bin midpoint)", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),

    # 🔹 Remove y-axis completely
    axis.text.y  = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank()
  )

print(p)
```
##### Improved version

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)

# ---- families to plot ----
families9 <- c(
  "Rhizosoleniaceae",
  "Chaetoceraceae",
  "Ceratiaceae",
  "Coccolithaceae",
  "Dictyochaceae",
  "Leptocylindraceae",
  "Noelaerhabdaceae",
  "Rhabdosphaeraceae",
  "Prorocentraceae"
)

# ---- palette + named colours ----
my_colors <- c(
  "#66C2A5", "#FC8D62", "#8DA0CB", "#E78AC3", "#FFD92F",
  "#E5C494", "#B3B3B3", "#A65628", "#FFFFB3"
)

fam_cols <- setNames(my_colors[1:length(families9)], families9)

# ---- helper: phyloseq -> long df ----
ps_to_long_df <- function(ps_obj, approach_name, families_keep,
                          lon_var = NULL, tax_rank = "Family") {
  
  stopifnot(inherits(ps_obj, "phyloseq"))
  
  otu <- as(otu_table(ps_obj), "matrix")
  if (!taxa_are_rows(ps_obj)) otu <- t(otu)
  otu <- (otu > 0) * 1
  
  tax <- as(tax_table(ps_obj), "matrix") %>%
    as.data.frame(check.names = FALSE)
  tax$.taxa_id <- rownames(tax)
  
  sam <- as(sample_data(ps_obj), "data.frame") %>%
    rownames_to_column("SampleID")
  
  names(sam) <- trimws(names(sam))
  
  if (is.null(lon_var)) {
    lon_candidates <- names(sam)[grepl("^lon(gitude)?$", tolower(names(sam)))]
    
    if (length(lon_candidates) == 0) {
      lon_candidates <- names(sam)[grepl("lon|long", tolower(names(sam)))]
    }
    
    if (length(lon_candidates) == 0) {
      stop("Could not find a longitude column in sample_data(ps_obj).")
    }
    
    lon_var <- lon_candidates[1]
  }
  
  if (!tax_rank %in% names(tax)) {
    stop(sprintf(
      "Tax table column '%s' not found. Available ranks are: %s",
      tax_rank, paste(names(tax), collapse = ", ")
    ))
  }
  
  otu_long <- as.data.frame(otu) %>%
    rownames_to_column(".taxa_id") %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = -".taxa_id",
      names_to = "SampleID",
      values_to = "pa"
    )
  
  otu_long %>%
    left_join(tax, by = ".taxa_id") %>%
    left_join(sam, by = "SampleID") %>%
    mutate(
      Family = as.character(.data[[tax_rank]]),
      approach = approach_name,
      longitude = as.numeric(.data[[lon_var]])
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Family), Family %in% families_keep) %>%
    select(approach, SampleID, longitude, Family, pa)
}

# ---- longitude binning settings ----
lon_min <- -16.149
lon_max <- -1.246
n_bins  <- 10

lon_breaks <- seq(lon_min, lon_max, length.out = n_bins + 1)

# ---- build long presence/absence dataframe ----
df_long_phyto <- bind_rows(
  ps_to_long_df(ps_cpr_pa,  "CPR",  families9, lon_var = NULL, tax_rank = "Family"),
  ps_to_long_df(ps_edna_pa, "eDNA", families9, lon_var = NULL, tax_rank = "Family")
) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(longitude),
    longitude >= lon_min,
    longitude <= lon_max
  )

# ---- collapse to family-level presence/absence per sample ----
df_sample_family_pa <- df_long_phyto %>%
  group_by(approach, SampleID, longitude, Family) %>%
  summarise(
    pa = as.integer(any(pa == 1)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ---- binned presence/absence ----
df_binned_phyto <- df_sample_family_pa %>%
  mutate(
    lon_bin = cut(
      longitude,
      breaks = lon_breaks,
      include.lowest = TRUE,
      right = TRUE
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(approach, lon_bin, Family) %>%
  summarise(
    pa = as.integer(any(pa == 1)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    approach = factor(approach, levels = c("CPR", "eDNA"))
  )

# ---- prevalence across longitude bins ----
prevalence_phyto_df <- df_binned_phyto %>%
  group_by(approach, Family) %>%
  summarise(
    n_present_bins = sum(pa == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_bins = n_distinct(lon_bin),
    prevalence = 100 * n_present_bins / n_bins,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ---- order families by overall prevalence across both methods ----
family_levels <- prevalence_phyto_df %>%
  group_by(Family) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_prevalence = mean(prevalence, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_prevalence), Family) %>%
  pull(Family)

df_binned_phyto <- df_binned_phyto %>%
  mutate(
    Family = factor(Family, levels = family_levels),
    fill_family = ifelse(pa == 1, as.character(Family), NA)
  )

prevalence_phyto_df <- prevalence_phyto_df %>%
  mutate(
    Family = factor(Family, levels = family_levels),
    approach = factor(approach, levels = c("CPR", "eDNA"))
  )

fam_cols_ranked <- fam_cols[family_levels]

# ---- x-axis labels: bin midpoints ----
bin_mids <- (lon_breaks[-1] + lon_breaks[-length(lon_breaks)]) / 2
mid_labels <- sprintf("%.1f", bin_mids)
names(mid_labels) <- levels(df_binned_phyto$lon_bin)

# ---- heatmap panel ----
p_heatmap_phyto <- ggplot(df_binned_phyto, aes(x = lon_bin, y = Family, fill = fill_family)) +
  geom_tile(width = 0.95, height = 0.9, colour = "grey80", linewidth = 0.25) +
  facet_wrap(~ approach, ncol = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = fam_cols_ranked,
    na.value = "white",
    breaks = family_levels,
    limits = family_levels,
    drop = FALSE,
    name = "Family"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = mid_labels) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(family_levels)) +
  labs(
    x = "Longitude section",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

# ---- prevalence barplot panel ----
p_prev_phyto <- ggplot(prevalence_phyto_df, aes(x = prevalence, y = Family)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, fill = "grey50") +
  facet_wrap(~ approach, ncol = 1) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(family_levels)) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    breaks = c(0, 50, 100),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Prevalence across\nlongitude bins (%)",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank()
  )

# ---- combine heatmap and prevalence barplot ----
p_phyto_final <- p_heatmap_phyto + p_prev_phyto +
  plot_layout(widths = c(4.5, 1.2), guides = "collect") +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Phytoplankton family detection across longitude sections"
  ) &
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14)
  )

p_phyto_final
```

#### Prevalence scatterplot

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggrepel)

# ---- combine colours ----
taxon_cols <- c(genus_cols, fam_cols)

# ---- zooplankton prevalence: wide format ----
zoo_scatter_df <- prevalence_df %>%
  select(approach, taxon = Genus, prevalence) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = approach,
    values_from = prevalence,
    values_fill = 0
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    group = "Zooplankton"
  )

# ---- phytoplankton prevalence: wide format ----
phyto_scatter_df <- prevalence_phyto_df %>%
  select(approach, taxon = Family, prevalence) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = approach,
    values_from = prevalence,
    values_fill = 0
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    group = "Phytoplankton"
  )

# ---- combine ----
scatter_df <- bind_rows(
  zoo_scatter_df,
  phyto_scatter_df
) %>%
  mutate(
    group = factor(group, levels = c("Phytoplankton", "Zooplankton")),
    taxon = as.character(taxon)
  )

# ---- scatterplot ----
p_scatter <- ggplot(
  scatter_df,
  aes(
    x = CPR,
    y = eDNA,
    colour = taxon,
    shape = group,
    label = taxon
  )
) +
  geom_abline(
    slope = 1,
    intercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed",
    colour = "grey60"
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.95) +
  geom_text_repel(
    size = 3,
    max.overlaps = Inf,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = taxon_cols,
    name = "Taxon"
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c(
      "Phytoplankton" = 16,  # full circle
      "Zooplankton" = 17     # triangle
    ),
    name = "Group"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, 25),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.05))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, 25),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.05))
  ) +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(
    x = "Prevalence in CPR (%)",
    y = "Prevalence in eDNA (%)",
    title = "Prevalence of dominant taxa in CPR and eDNA datasets"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

p_scatter
```

#### Prevalence scatterplot with all 40 shared genus

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)

# ---- shared genera ----
shared_genera <- intersect(
  get_taxa_at_rank(ps_edna_pa, "Genus"),
  get_taxa_at_rank(ps_cpr_pa, "Genus")
)

length(shared_genera)

# ---- robust prevalence function ----
get_genus_prevalence <- function(ps_obj, approach_name, genera_keep) {
  
  otu <- as(otu_table(ps_obj), "matrix")
  if (!taxa_are_rows(ps_obj)) otu <- t(otu)
  otu <- (otu > 0) * 1
  
  tax <- as(tax_table(ps_obj), "matrix") %>%
    as.data.frame(check.names = FALSE) %>%
    rownames_to_column(".taxa_id")
  
  sample_ids <- sample_names(ps_obj)
  
  otu_long <- as.data.frame(otu) %>%
    rownames_to_column(".taxa_id") %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = -".taxa_id",
      names_to = "SampleID",
      values_to = "pa"
    ) %>%
    left_join(tax, by = ".taxa_id") %>%
    mutate(
      Genus = as.character(Genus)
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Genus), Genus %in% genera_keep) %>%
    group_by(SampleID, Genus) %>%
    summarise(
      pa = as.integer(any(pa == 1)),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    complete(
      SampleID = sample_ids,
      Genus = genera_keep,
      fill = list(pa = 0)
    ) %>%
    group_by(Genus) %>%
    summarise(
      n_present = sum(pa == 1),
      n_samples = n_distinct(SampleID),
      prevalence = 100 * n_present / n_samples,
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      approach = approach_name
    )
  
  otu_long
}

# ---- calculate prevalence separately for each method ----
prev_cpr <- get_genus_prevalence(
  ps_obj = ps_cpr_pa,
  approach_name = "CPR",
  genera_keep = shared_genera
)

prev_edna <- get_genus_prevalence(
  ps_obj = ps_edna_pa,
  approach_name = "eDNA",
  genera_keep = shared_genera
)

# ---- combine into wide format ----
genus_prev_df <- bind_rows(prev_cpr, prev_edna) %>%
  select(approach, Genus, prevalence) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = approach,
    values_from = prevalence
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    difference = eDNA - CPR
  )

# ---- correlation test ----
cor_test <- cor.test(
  genus_prev_df$CPR,
  genus_prev_df$eDNA,
  method = "spearman"
)

rho_label <- paste0(
  "n = ", nrow(genus_prev_df),
  "\nSpearman \u03c1 = ", round(unname(cor_test$estimate), 2),
  "\np = ", signif(cor_test$p.value, 2)
)

# ---- how many genera are more prevalent in eDNA ----
n_edna_higher <- sum(genus_prev_df$eDNA > genus_prev_df$CPR)
perc_edna_higher <- 100 * mean(genus_prev_df$eDNA > genus_prev_df$CPR)

edna_label <- paste0(
  n_edna_higher, "/", nrow(genus_prev_df),
  " genera higher in eDNA\n(",
  round(perc_edna_higher, 1), "%)"
)

# ---- improved scatterplot ----
p_fig2b_clean <- ggplot(genus_prev_df, aes(x = CPR, y = eDNA)) +
  geom_abline(
    slope = 1,
    intercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed",
    colour = "grey60",
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_point(
    size = 3,
    alpha = 0.7,
    colour = "black"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, 25),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.04))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, 25),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.04))
  ) +
  coord_equal() +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 98,
    y = 5,
    label = rho_label,
    hjust = 1,
    vjust = 0,
    size = 4
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 98,
    y = 18,
    label = edna_label,
    hjust = 1,
    vjust = 0,
    size = 4
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Prevalence in CPR samples (%)",
    y = "Prevalence in eDNA samples (%)",
    title = "Comparison of genus prevalence between CPR and eDNA"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

p_fig2b_clean
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)

# ---- classify genera from taxonomy table ----
classify_shared_genera <- function(ps_obj, shared_genera) {
  
  tax <- as(tax_table(ps_obj), "matrix") %>%
    as.data.frame(check.names = FALSE)
  
  tax %>%
    mutate(
      Genus = as.character(Genus),
      Phylum = as.character(Phylum),
      Class = as.character(Class),
      Order = as.character(Order),
      Family = as.character(Family)
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Genus), Genus %in% shared_genera) %>%
    distinct(Genus, Phylum, Class, Order, Family) %>%
    mutate(
      broad_group = case_when(
        # Zooplankton / metazoans
        Phylum %in% c("Arthropoda", "Cnidaria", "Ctenophora", "Mollusca",
                      "Annelida", "Chaetognatha", "Chordata", "Rotifera", "Ciliophora") ~ "Zooplankton",
        
        # Common phytoplankton groups
        Phylum %in% c("Bacillariophyta", "Dinoflagellata", "Haptophyta",
                      "Ochrophyta", "Chlorophyta", "Cryptophyta",
                      "Cyanobacteria") ~ "Phytoplankton",
        
        Class %in% c("Mediophyceae", "Bacillariophyceae", "Coscinodiscophyceae",
                     "Dinophyceae", "Prymnesiophyceae", "Dictyochophyceae",
                     "Chlorophyceae", "Cryptophyceae") ~ "Phytoplankton",
        
        TRUE ~ "Other/ambiguous"
      )
    ) %>%
    group_by(Genus) %>%
    summarise(
      broad_group = case_when(
        any(broad_group == "Zooplankton") ~ "Zooplankton",
        any(broad_group == "Phytoplankton") ~ "Phytoplankton",
        TRUE ~ "Other/ambiguous"
      ),
      taxonomy_summary = paste(
        unique(na.omit(paste(Phylum, Class, sep = " / "))),
        collapse = "; "
      ),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}

# ---- classify using both datasets, then combine ----
genus_groups <- bind_rows(
  classify_shared_genera(ps_cpr_pa, shared_genera),
  classify_shared_genera(ps_edna_pa, shared_genera)
) %>%
  group_by(Genus) %>%
  summarise(
    broad_group = case_when(
      any(broad_group == "Zooplankton") ~ "Zooplankton",
      any(broad_group == "Phytoplankton") ~ "Phytoplankton",
      TRUE ~ "Other/ambiguous"
    ),
    taxonomy_summary = paste(unique(taxonomy_summary), collapse = "; "),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Check classification
genus_groups %>%
  count(broad_group)

genus_groups %>%
  filter(broad_group == "Other/ambiguous")
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
genus_prev_df_grouped <- genus_prev_df %>%
  left_join(genus_groups, by = c("Genus" = "Genus")) %>%
  mutate(
    broad_group = replace_na(broad_group, "Other/ambiguous")
  )

# ---- correlation test ----
cor_test <- cor.test(
  genus_prev_df_grouped$CPR,
  genus_prev_df_grouped$eDNA,
  method = "spearman"
)

rho_label <- paste0(
  "n = ", nrow(genus_prev_df_grouped),
  "\nSpearman \u03c1 = ", round(unname(cor_test$estimate), 2),
  "\np = ", signif(cor_test$p.value, 2)
)

# ---- number of genera higher in eDNA ----
n_edna_higher <- sum(genus_prev_df_grouped$eDNA > genus_prev_df_grouped$CPR)
perc_edna_higher <- 100 * mean(genus_prev_df_grouped$eDNA > genus_prev_df_grouped$CPR)

edna_label <- paste0(
  n_edna_higher, "/", nrow(genus_prev_df_grouped),
  " genera higher in eDNA\n(",
  round(perc_edna_higher, 1), "%)"
)

# ---- plot ----
p_fig2b_grouped <- ggplot(
  genus_prev_df_grouped,
  aes(x = CPR, y = eDNA, colour = broad_group)
) +
  geom_abline(
    slope = 1,
    intercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed",
    colour = "grey60",
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_point(
    size = 3.2,
    alpha = 0.75
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c(
      "Zooplankton" = "#377EB8",
      "Phytoplankton" = "#4DAF4A",
      "Other/ambiguous" = "grey60"
    ),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, 25),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.04))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, 25),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.04))
  ) +
  coord_equal() +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 98,
    y = 5,
    label = rho_label,
    hjust = 1,
    vjust = 0,
    size = 4
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Prevalence in CPR samples (%)",
    y = "Prevalence in eDNA samples (%)",
    title = "Comparison of genus prevalence between CPR and eDNA"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

p_fig2b_grouped
```

### NMDS

#### CPR

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(tidyverse)

otu_cpr <- otu_table(ps_cpr)
tax_cpr <- tax_table(ps_cpr)
sam_cpr <- sample_data(ps_cpr) 

# force taxonomy order to match taxa order 
tax_cpr <- tax_cpr[taxa_names(ps_cpr), ] 

# rebuild object
ps_cpr_fixed <- phyloseq(otu_cpr, tax_cpr, sam_cpr)

# ---- extract CPR community matrix ----
mat_cpr <- as(otu_table(ps_cpr_fixed), "matrix")

# samples should be rows, taxa should be columns
if (taxa_are_rows(ps_cpr_fixed)) {
  mat_cpr <- t(mat_cpr)
}

# ---- convert to presence/absence ----
mat_cpr_pa <- (mat_cpr > 0) * 1

# remove taxa absent from all samples
mat_cpr_pa <- mat_cpr_pa[, colSums(mat_cpr_pa) > 0, drop = FALSE]

# remove samples with no taxa detected, if any
mat_cpr_pa <- mat_cpr_pa[rowSums(mat_cpr_pa) > 0, , drop = FALSE]

# ---- NMDS using Jaccard distance ----
set.seed(123)

nmds_cpr <- metaMDS(
  mat_cpr_pa,
  distance = "jaccard",
  binary = TRUE,
  k = 2,
  trymax = 200,
  autotransform = FALSE,
  trace = FALSE
)

# ---- extract NMDS scores ----
scores_cpr <- as.data.frame(scores(nmds_cpr, display = "sites")) %>%
  rownames_to_column("SampleID")

# ---- extract metadata ----
meta_cpr <- as(sample_data(ps_cpr_fixed), "data.frame") %>%
  rownames_to_column("SampleID")

# keep only samples used in NMDS
meta_cpr <- meta_cpr %>%
  filter(SampleID %in% rownames(mat_cpr_pa))

cat("Columns in meta_cpr:\n")
print(colnames(meta_cpr))

cat("Columns in scores_cpr:\n")
print(colnames(scores_cpr))

plot_cpr <- scores_cpr %>%
  left_join(meta_cpr, by = "SampleID")

cat("Columns in plot_cpr after join:\n")
print(colnames(plot_cpr))

plot_cpr <- scores_cpr %>%
  left_join(meta_cpr, by = "SampleID")

print(colnames(plot_cpr))
print(head(plot_cpr$Water_masses))
stopifnot("Water_masses" %in% colnames(plot_cpr))

ggplot(
  plot_cpr,
  aes(
    x = NMDS1,
    y = NMDS2,
    colour = Water_masses
  )
) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  stat_ellipse(
    aes(group = Water_masses),
    linewidth = 0.8,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c(
      "Open Ocean" = "#D55E00",
      "Shelf" = "#0072B2"
    )
  ) +
  coord_equal(
    xlim = c(-1.3, 1.0),
    ylim = c(-1.3, 1.0)
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
```
```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
adonis2(
  mat_cpr_pa ~ Water_masses,
  data = meta_cpr,
  method = "jaccard",
  binary = TRUE,
  permutations = 999
)
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
dist_cpr <- vegdist(
  mat_cpr_pa,
  method = "jaccard",
  binary = TRUE
)

disp_cpr <- betadisper(
  dist_cpr,
  meta_cpr$Water_masses
)

anova(disp_cpr)
permutest(disp_cpr)
```

#### eDNA

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(tidyverse)

# ---- extract matrix ----
mat_edna <- as(otu_table(ps_edna), "matrix")

if (taxa_are_rows(ps_edna)) {
  mat_edna <- t(mat_edna)
}

# ---- presence / absence ----
mat_edna_pa <- (mat_edna > 0) * 1

# remove empty taxa
mat_edna_pa <- mat_edna_pa[, colSums(mat_edna_pa) > 0, drop = FALSE]

# remove empty samples
mat_edna_pa <- mat_edna_pa[rowSums(mat_edna_pa) > 0, , drop = FALSE]

dim(mat_edna_pa)

set.seed(123)

nmds_edna <- metaMDS(
  mat_edna_pa,
  distance = "jaccard",
  binary = TRUE,
  k = 2,
  trymax = 200,
  autotransform = FALSE,
  trace = FALSE
)

nmds_edna$stress

scores_edna <- as.data.frame(
  scores(nmds_edna, display = "sites")
) %>%
  rownames_to_column("SampleID")

meta_edna <- as(sample_data(ps_edna), "data.frame") %>%
  rownames_to_column("SampleID")

plot_edna <- left_join(
  scores_edna,
  meta_edna,
  by = "SampleID"
)

ggplot(
  plot_edna,
  aes(
    NMDS1,
    NMDS2,
    colour = Water_masses
  )
) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  stat_ellipse(
    aes(group = Water_masses),
    linewidth = 0.8,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c(
      "Open Ocean" = "#D55E00",
      "Shelf" = "#0072B2"
    )
  ) +
  coord_equal(
    xlim = c(-3.8, 2.0),
    ylim = c(-1.6, 2.4)
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

```
```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
adonis2(
  mat_edna_pa ~ Water_masses,
  data = meta_edna,
  method = "jaccard",
  binary = TRUE,
  permutations = 999
)
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
dist_edna <- vegdist(
  mat_edna_pa,
  method = "jaccard",
  binary = TRUE
)

disp_edna <- betadisper(
  dist_edna,
  meta_edna$Water_masses
)

anova(disp_edna)
permutest(disp_edna)
```

#### eDNA NMDS at genus level

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(tidyverse)

# -------------------------
# Aggregate to Genus level
# -------------------------

ps_edna_genus <- tax_glom(
  ps_edna,
  taxrank = "Genus",
  NArm = TRUE
)

# -------------------------
# Extract matrix
# -------------------------

mat_genus <- as(otu_table(ps_edna_genus), "matrix")

if (taxa_are_rows(ps_edna_genus)) {
  mat_genus <- t(mat_genus)
}

# -------------------------
# Presence / absence
# -------------------------

mat_genus_pa <- (mat_genus > 0) * 1

# Remove empty taxa
mat_genus_pa <- mat_genus_pa[
  ,
  colSums(mat_genus_pa) > 0,
  drop = FALSE
]

# Remove empty samples
mat_genus_pa <- mat_genus_pa[
  rowSums(mat_genus_pa) > 0,
  ,
  drop = FALSE
]

dim(mat_genus_pa)

# -------------------------
# NMDS
# -------------------------

set.seed(123)

nmds_edna_genus <- metaMDS(
  mat_genus_pa,
  distance = "jaccard",
  binary = TRUE,
  k = 2,
  trymax = 200,
  autotransform = FALSE,
  trace = FALSE
)

nmds_edna_genus$stress

scores_edna_genus <- as.data.frame(
  scores(nmds_edna_genus, display = "sites")
) %>%
  rownames_to_column("SampleID")

meta_edna <- as(sample_data(ps_edna), "data.frame") %>%
  rownames_to_column("SampleID")

plot_edna_genus <- left_join(
  scores_edna_genus,
  meta_edna,
  by = "SampleID"
)

p_edna_genus <- ggplot(
  plot_edna_genus,
  aes(
    NMDS1,
    NMDS2,
    colour = Water_masses
  )
) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  stat_ellipse(
    aes(group = Water_masses),
    linewidth = 0.8,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c(
      "Open Ocean" = "#D55E00",
      "Shelf" = "#0072B2"
    )
  ) +
  coord_equal(
    xlim = c(-1.3, 1.0),
    ylim = c(-1.3, 1.0)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "eDNA (Metabarcoding)",
    colour = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

p_edna_genus
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
adonis2(
  mat_genus_pa ~ Water_masses,
  data = meta_edna,
  method = "jaccard",
  binary = TRUE,
  permutations = 999
)
```


```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
dist_genus <- vegdist(
  mat_genus_pa,
  method = "jaccard",
  binary = TRUE
)

disp_genus <- betadisper(
  dist_genus,
  meta_edna$Water_masses
)

anova(disp_genus)
permutest(disp_genus)
```

### Unique to CPR vs unique to eDNA

```{r genus-overlap-counts, cache=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(phyloseq)

# -----------------------------
# 1. Helper: extract genera present in a phyloseq object
# -----------------------------

get_present_genera <- function(ps, ps_name = "phyloseq object") {
  
  if (!exists("tax_table", mode = "function")) {
    stop("phyloseq is not loaded.")
  }
  
  if (!inherits(ps, "phyloseq")) {
    stop(ps_name, " is not a phyloseq object.")
  }
  
  tax <- as(tax_table(ps), "matrix") %>%
    as.data.frame(check.names = FALSE)
  
  cat("\nChecking", ps_name, "\n")
  cat("Tax table columns:\n")
  print(colnames(tax))
  
  if (!"Genus" %in% colnames(tax)) {
    stop(ps_name, " does not contain a 'Genus' column in its taxonomy table.")
  }
  
  present_taxa <- taxa_names(ps)[taxa_sums(ps) > 0]
  present_taxa <- intersect(present_taxa, rownames(tax))
  
  if (length(present_taxa) == 0) {
    stop("No matching present taxa found between taxa_names(", ps_name, ") and rownames(tax_table).")
  }
  
  tax[present_taxa, "Genus", drop = TRUE] %>%
    as.character() %>%
    discard(~ is.na(.x) | .x == "") %>%
    unique()
}

edna_genera <- get_present_genera(ps_edna, "ps_edna")
cpr_genera  <- get_present_genera(ps_cpr, "ps_cpr")

shared_genera <- intersect(edna_genera, cpr_genera)
cpr_only_genera <- setdiff(cpr_genera, edna_genera)
edna_only_genera <- setdiff(edna_genera, cpr_genera)

genus_overlap_counts <- tibble(
  category = c("Shared", "CPR only", "eDNA only"),
  n_genera = c(
    length(shared_genera),
    length(cpr_only_genera),
    length(edna_only_genera)
  )
)

genus_overlap_counts
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# -----------------------------
# 3. Helper: calculate genus prevalence within one method
# -----------------------------

calculate_genus_prevalence <- function(ps, genera_keep) {

  otu <- as(otu_table(ps), "matrix")
  if (!taxa_are_rows(ps)) otu <- t(otu)

  otu <- (otu > 0) * 1

  tax <- as(tax_table(ps), "matrix") %>%
    as.data.frame(check.names = FALSE) %>%
    rownames_to_column(".taxa_id")

  sample_ids <- sample_names(ps)

  as.data.frame(otu) %>%
    rownames_to_column(".taxa_id") %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = -".taxa_id",
      names_to = "SampleID",
      values_to = "pa"
    ) %>%
    left_join(tax, by = ".taxa_id") %>%
    mutate(
      Genus = as.character(Genus)
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Genus), Genus %in% genera_keep) %>%
    group_by(SampleID, Genus) %>%
    summarise(
      pa = as.integer(any(pa == 1)),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    complete(
      SampleID = sample_ids,
      Genus = genera_keep,
      fill = list(pa = 0)
    ) %>%
    group_by(Genus) %>%
    summarise(
      n_present = sum(pa == 1),
      n_samples = n_distinct(SampleID),
      prevalence = 100 * n_present / n_samples,
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(prevalence))
}

# -----------------------------
# 4. Most prevalent CPR-only genera
# -----------------------------

cpr_only_prev <- calculate_genus_prevalence(
  ps = ps_cpr,
  genera_keep = cpr_only_genera
) %>%
  mutate(category = "CPR only")

cpr_only_prev %>%
  arrange(desc(prevalence)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20)

```

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

# -----------------------------
# 5. Most prevalent eDNA-only genera
# -----------------------------

edna_only_prev <- calculate_genus_prevalence(
  ps = ps_edna,
  genera_keep = edna_only_genera
) %>%
  mutate(category = "eDNA only")

edna_only_prev %>%
  arrange(desc(prevalence)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20)
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidytext)
top_method_specific <- bind_rows(
  cpr_only_prev %>% slice_max(prevalence, n = 10),
  edna_only_prev %>% slice_max(prevalence, n = 10)
) %>%
  mutate(
    Genus = reorder_within(Genus, prevalence, category)
  )

ggplot(
  top_method_specific,
  aes(x = prevalence, y = Genus, fill = category)
) +
  geom_col(width = 0.75) +
  facet_wrap(~ category, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_y_reordered() +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, 25)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Prevalence within method (%)",
    y = NULL,
    fill = NULL,
    title = "Most prevalent method-specific genera"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none"
  )
```

### Next

- Presence/absence by region: Does agreement improve in high-biomass / high-diversity regions? --> Heatmap for the 10 copepods?
- comparing ecological groups: Zooplankton/phytoplankton only
- Sensitivity analysis: e.g. taxa present in ≥2 samples


**A spectrum of biodiversity resolution**

These methods can be viewed as sampling different dimensions of diversity along a continuum of biological detail:

CPR (morphological counts) primarily captures → large-bodied, abundant, and morphologically distinctive taxa → reflects biomass-dominant and ecologically conspicuous organisms → integrates over time and space due to net sampling and tow length

eDNA metabarcoding (18S, COI) captures → small, fragile, rare, cryptic, or early life stages → reflects presence and relative diversity, not biomass → is sensitive to extracellular DNA, transport, and detection limits

Because these approaches target different components of the community, direct one-to-one taxonomic comparisons are not always meaningful.

![Resolution pyramid - CPR vs RoCSI eDNA](CPR%20vs%20eDNA%20biological%20resolution%20comparison.png){width="80%"}

![Horizontal gradient of ecological resolution](ChatGPT%20Image%20Jan%206,%202026,%2003_15_35%20PM.png)
